steps.title: Recognize slope-intercept form
steps.content: For a linear function, the $y$-intercept is the constant term when the function is written in slope-intercept form.

steps.title: Identify the constant in the given function
steps.content: In $f(x) = -9x + 9$, the constant term (the number not multiplied by $x$) is 9. Therefore, the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept is 9.
